title: Body-Safe Certification 101 for Couples

hook_slide.headline: Is Your Intimacy Truly Safe?
hook_slide.subline: Discover the secrets to truly body-safe products for deeper connection.
hook_slide.image_concept: A softly lit, textured background with abstract, flowing shapes in warm, dark tones, evoking mystery and comfort. Minimalistic and elegant.

education_slides.headline: What is 'Body-Safe'?
education_slides.body: It means products are made with non-toxic, non-irritating materials, designed to be used internally or externally without causing harm. Essential for intimate wellness.
education_slides.image_concept: Minimalist design with a single, smooth, abstract form, suggesting safety and purity, against a soft, muted background.

education_slides.headline: Why Does It Matter?
education_slides.body: Your intimate areas are highly absorbent and sensitive. Harmful chemicals can disrupt natural pH, cause irritation, or lead to long-term health issues.
education_slides.image_concept: A gentle gradient transitioning from a slightly darker hue to a lighter, softer one, symbolizing vulnerability and protection.

education_slides.headline: The 'Porous vs. Non-Porous' Rule
education_slides.body: Non-porous materials (silicone, glass, metal) are easier to clean and sanitize, reducing bacteria growth. Porous materials (TPR, jelly) can harbor germs.
education_slides.image_concept: A split image concept, one side smooth and reflective, the other slightly textured and matte, representing the two material types.

education_slides.headline: Silicone: The Gold Standard
education_slides.body: Look for 100% 'platinum-cured' or 'medical-grade' silicone. It's durable, non-porous, hypoallergenic, and free from phthalates and latex.
education_slides.image_concept: A clean, elegant close-up of a smooth, rounded form in a soft, muted color, highlighting its flawless texture and purity.

education_slides.headline: Avoid These Materials
education_slides.body: Steer clear of 'jelly,' 'TPR,' 'PVC,' or 'phthalate-laden' plastics. These can degrade, leach chemicals, and are difficult to sanitize properly.
education_slides.image_concept: A slightly blurred background with a subtle 'X' overlay, indicating caution without being aggressive, using a darker color palette.

education_slides.headline: Lubricant Compatibility
education_slides.body: Always check lube compatibility with your toys. Water-based is generally safe for all. Oil-based can degrade silicone, and silicone-based can degrade silicone.
education_slides.image_concept: Two abstract, interlocking shapes, one smooth and one slightly textured, suggesting interaction and compatibility, in warm, inviting colors.

education_slides.headline: Certifications & Transparency
education_slides.body: Reputable brands often disclose materials and manufacturing processes. Look for certifications or clear statements about body-safe practices.
education_slides.image_concept: A minimalist design featuring a subtle, abstract 'seal' or 'stamp' icon, implying trust and verification, against a clean background.

framework_slide.title: Your Body-Safe Product Checklist
items: Is it 100% platinum-cured silicone, glass, or metal?
Is it free from phthalates, latex, and BPA?
Is it easy to clean and sanitize thoroughly?
Is it compatible with your chosen lubricant?
Does the brand disclose material information clearly?
framework_slide.image_concept: A structured layout with clean lines and subtle checkmark icons, using a calming color palette of soft blues and warm grays.

cta_slide.headline: Curated by Dark Fantasy • bdsmpub.com
cta_slide.subline: First link in caption for deeper dives into intimate wellness.
cta_slide.image_concept: A minimalist Dark Fantasy logo subtly placed on a warm, inviting, deep textured background, perhaps with a soft glow.
